executor_handoff.system_instructions: Preserve existing patterns. Follow scope exactly. Do NOT widen scope. Use ONLY the chosen path below. Readonly elsewhere. Swift 6 strict concurrency. macOS14+. Use serena for edits, xcode-tools for build/test. Project conventions (styleguide.md / unit-testing.md) override any skill defaults.

task_brief.objective: Relabel per-refresh stdout line: when a live playlist refresh found NO change AND playlist still healthy AND zero findings this cycle, replace the green "✓ OK Refreshed : no findings." line with a WHITE notice: "↻ HOLD Refreshed : didn't change after <waited>s -> re-try in <next>s". Healthy-only. Everything else unchanged.
task_brief.why: A refresh that returns identical media after a full target-duration wait is the first sign of a possible stall (RFC 8216 §6.3.4 cadence). Green "OK" overstates health. White HOLD note flags it without raising a finding, distinct from green OK and dim findings.
task_brief.repo_signals: - SessionEvent enum: ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Session/SessionConfig.swift:136-163 case refreshCompleted(playlistID:index:errors:warnings:) @159. Marked "US2 additive — no JSON/exit impact". - Emit site + monitor loop: ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Session/ValidationSession+Monitoring.swift loop @48-183; `changed` @129; `delay` @54; `scheduler` @52; emit(.refreshCompleted) @167-172. scheduler.nextDelay(didChange:) = changed ? target : target/2 (RefreshScheduler.swift:42-43). - Renderer (text): Valistream/Valistream/Valistream/StatusRenderer.swift renderRefreshCompleted @294-318 (OK branch @307); render[0] dispatch @86-93. seconds(_:) @469 returns BARE number string ("4","2","2.5") — no "s" suffix. - Renderer (JSON): same file render[1] @120-123 (machine path). - Markers: Valistream/Valistream/Valistream/TerminalWriter.swift marker(for:) @141-150, successMarker() @152-154 (✓ OK / [OK]). apply(style:) @242 has .white="37". - Role→color: ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Output/PresentationRole.swift .notice → .white @41-42 ("attention"). USE .notice — already exists, no new role. - Staleness: StalenessDetector.swift — strict `>`: warn stale>1.5×target, error stale>3×target. - IncidentTimeline.swift:117 — refreshCompleted grouped-ignored.
task_brief.scope: IN: - Add RefreshHold value type (ValistreamCore). - Add hold param to SessionEvent.refreshCompleted (additive 5th value). - Compute hold in monitor loop emit gate. - New first branch in renderRefreshCompleted rendering the HOLD line, role .notice. - Add writer.holdMarker() → "↻ HOLD" / "[HOLD]". - Fix all match sites (render[0], render[1], IncidentTimeline, any other exhaustive switch). OUT (do NOT touch): - Stale no-change refreshes (post-WARN/ERROR, monitorState != .monitoring) still hit existing green "✓ OK no findings" branch. KNOWN pre-existing wart. Leave as-is. - JSON output shape: bind hold as `_`. No new JSON field. No exit-code change. - Staleness logic, RefreshScheduler, verbose trace events (.refreshScheduled/.refreshRetry).
task_brief.code_level_guidance: - Wording EXACT: "↻ HOLD Refreshed <snapshot>: didn't change after <waited>s -> re-try in <next>s". Use literal arrow "-> " (ASCII). seconds() yields bare number, append "s" in the string. - Cadence geometry (target T): after a change, wait T to next refresh; after no-change, wait T/2. So healthy-no-change line normally fires on refresh #2 (waited = full T). Practically the only case (real-clock jitter pushes #3 over 1.5×T → WARN → not a hold). Under a deterministic test clock #3 may slip (staleFor==1.5×T, strict `>`) → waited = T/2 → line prints "after 2s". That is intended/honest — do NOT hardcode "target duration". - hold gate is authoritative in the loop. monitorState(for:) at emit reflects this refresh's staleness eval (evaluateStaleness @154-162 runs BEFORE emit). Healthy ⟺ .monitoring. - waited = `delay` (the Duration slept this iteration). nextRetry = scheduler.nextDelay(didChange:false)=T/2. - role .notice → white. Do NOT invent a new PresentationRole.
task_brief.search_fallback: grep ".refreshCompleted" and "case refreshCompleted" across *.swift to find ALL match sites. grep "successMarker" for marker pattern. grep "enum SessionEvent" if file moved.
task_brief.acceptance_criteria: - Healthy no-change refresh, 0 findings, normal verbosity → line: "↻ HOLD Refreshed <id>: didn't change after 4s -> re-try in 2s" (target=4s), styled white (.notice). - ASCII glyph mode → "[HOLD]". - Changed refresh with 0 findings → unchanged "✓ OK ... no findings.". - Refresh with findings → unchanged warnings/errors line. - Stale (WARN/ERROR already) no-change refresh → NOT a HOLD line (falls to existing branch). - quiet verbosity → HOLD line suppressed (same guard as OK line). - JSON mode → byte-identical to before (hold ignored). Exit codes unchanged. - Existing readability/distinctness tests still green; update fixtures/expectations ONLY where a scenario now legitimately produces a HOLD line instead of OK (check VerboseDistinctnessTests:256, NormalSessionReadabilityTests, QuietModeFindingsTests, IncidentTimelineReportTests).
task_brief.validation: - Build scheme `Valistream` (xcode-tools BuildProject) → 0 errors. - Run `Valistream.xctestplan` (RunAllTests) → all pass. - Add focused Swift Testing coverage for the hold branch (text + ascii marker + healthy gate + stale-suppression), per unit-testing.md conventions.
